Wait, something strange happened. The magenta nodes now show:

Wait, I think the ids are assigned based on the top-most, left-most order. So after moving, the ids might have been reassigned. Let me check:
- id=9: [30-31, 34-35] - this is the square that was moved (originally id=10)
- id=10: [34-35, 15-16] - this is the square that was originally id=9
So the ids got swapped because the squares changed their relative positions. The square that was originally id=10 (at [35-36, 39-40]) moved to [30-31, 34-35], and since it's now above and to the left of the square that was originally id=9 (at [34-35, 15-16]), it got the lower id (9 instead of 10).
Wait, but [30-31, 34-35] is above [34-35, 15-16] (row 30 < row 34), so the square at [30-31, 34-35] should have a lower id. That makes sense.
But wait, the square at [34-35, 15-16] is to the left of [30-31, 34-35] (col 15 < col 34). So the id assignment is based on row first, then column. The square at row 30 comes before the square at row 34, so it gets the lower id.
OK, so the movement worked correctly. The square that was at [35-36, 39-40] moved to [30-31, 34-35] after 5 up-left moves.

When I clicked on [54, 13] (bottom-left corner of id=12 at [53-54, 13-14]), it moved to [53-54, 12-13]. So it moved left by 1 column, staying in the same row. This is "left" movement.

When I clicked on [54, 49] (bottom-right corner of id=11 at [53-54, 48-49]), it didn't move. This is "no move".
So the movement mechanics are:
- Top-left corner → up-left
- Top-right corner → up
- Bottom-left corner → left
- Bottom-right corner → no move
This means I can only move the squares in 3 directions: up-left, up, and left. I can't move them down or right.

- After moving id=10 (now id=9) up-left 5 times, it's now at [30-31, 34-35], overlapping with the blue blob at [23-31, 29-37].
- Movement confirmed: top-left→up-left, top-right→up, bottom-left→left, bottom-right→no move.
- Can only move squares up and/or left, not down or right.
- Current positions: id=9 [30-31, 34-35] (in blob), id=10 [34-35, 15-16], id=11 [52-53, 47-48], id=12 [53-54, 12-13]
- Only squares to the right of the blue blob can reach it (id=9 and id=11).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move id=11 at [52-53, 47-48] toward the blue blob [23-31, 29-37]
# Need to move up ~20 rows and left ~18 columns
# Use top-left corner clicks for up-left movement
# Start at [52, 47], move up-left 18 times to reach [34-35, 29-30]
moves = []
r, c = 52, 47
for i in range(18):
    moves.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c})
    r -= 1
    c -= 1

predict(expect_change=True, note='Move id=11 up-left 18 times toward blue blob')
action(moves)
print("Result:", last_action_result)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Magenta nodes after moves:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("Blue blob:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"  id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
